channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Military AI is here. Some experts are worried
item.description: For the first two-and-a-half years of the generative AI revolution, the AI arms race has been waged between competing companies seeking to make bank from the promise and potential of the technology. But things are maturing in the AI world—and with it, there’s another frontline for AI: the military.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge is being tipped to come with a sweet Google Gemini deal
item.description: This could be another reason to invest in the Samsung Galaxy S25 Edge, besides its super-thin design.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 7 of my favorite upgrades in the all-new Roomba robovacs – plus 2 I'm worried about
item.description: Improved mopping, coherent naming, and LiDAR at last – iRobot's new fleet of Roomba bots looks very promising.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 2 tasty new tab tricks for Chrome on Android
item.description: Quick: How many tabs do you have open in Chrome on your Android phone right now? If the answer is zero, you’re an animal. Seek immediate psychiatric help. If the answer is one to two, you’re obsessively organized and a model Android device owner.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Daredevil: Born Again episode 3's shocking final scene is a big misdirect, and I've got the evidence to back it up
item.description: That unexpectedly devastating moment in Daredevil: Born Again's third episode has nothing to do with a fan-favorite Marvel anti-hero.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: New figures claim the smartwatch market just shrunk for the first time ever, and the Apple Watch Ultra 3 is to blame
item.description: A new report claims smartwatch shipments have declined, and that waning Apple Watch interest is to blame.
item.dc:creator: stephen.warwick@futurenet.com (Stephen Warwick), TechRadar

item.title: Robot uses lasers to make chemical-free farming a reality
item.description: The LaserWeeder G2 uses lasers, artificial intelligence and advanced robotics to offer an alternative to chemicals traditionally used in farming.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Wasm fascinates me
item.description: WebAssembly fascinates me. The notion of assembly language is as old as computing. Typically, assembly meant specific instructions for moving data around inside of a physical central processor. Everything in a computer is ones and zeros, but a layer above that is assembly code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Culture and cloud combine to harness data at Regeneron
item.description: Having been CIO at Regeneron Pharmaceuticals for six years, and promoted to that role from within, Bob McCowan had the advantage to approach such an all encompassing position with an intimate knowledge of how the infrastructure runs.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 2025 CIO Awards New Zealand: Nominations now open
item.description: Nominations are now open for the 2025 CIO Awards New Zealand. The annual awards, presented by IDC and Brightstar with support from CIO New Zealand, celebrate individuals and teams showing exceptional leadership, innovation, and foresight in their contributions to ICT and business.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump is buying a Tesla after years of bashing EVs
item.description: President Donald Trump, who’s buying a Tesla to show loyalty to company CEO Elon Musk, has had plenty to say about electric vehicles over the years. Most of it is not good. Of course, Trump was once a sharp critic of Musk, too, which is especially notable given how tight the pair are now.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: The US agency that monitors weather will cut another 1,000 jobs, AP sources say
item.description: Multiple sources tell The Associated Press that the Trump Administration is starting another round of job cuts — this one more than 1,000 — at the nation’s weather, ocean and fisheries agency.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What the breakup of Google could mean for Apple and other tech companies
item.description: Alphabet may have hoped that Donald Trump winning the White House would quell talk of a Google breakup, but that doesn’t seem likely.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster
item.description: Rethinking the delivery process for new capabilities is essential to put cybersecurity and the warfighter front and center. The post How to deliver more secure capabilities to the warfighter, faster first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Asus might have just changed the display game for good with three new air-purifying monitors - and one of them is ultrawide
item.description: Asus' new monitors may not include any significant improvements to their display quality, but the Taiwanese company is bringing enhancements to indoor air quality in a unique way.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Two-thirds of Silicon Valley tech workers are foreign-born, new report reveals
item.description: Of tech workers with a bachelor's degree or higher, 23% came from India and 18% from China, while 17% were born in California, according to the Silicon Valley Index.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Weaviate adds agents to its tech stack to ease gen AI app development
item.description: Vector database provider Weaviate has added three new agents to its development stack to facilitate the development of generative AI-based applications. These agents are modular agentic workflows that use large language models (LLMs) and prompts to interact dynamically with data in Weaviate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Elon Musk claims X being targeted in 'massive cyberattack' as service goes down
item.description: Hours after a series of outages Monday that left X unavailable to thousands of users, Elon Musk claimed that the social media platform was being targeted in a “massive cyberattack.” “We get attacked every day, but this was done with a lot of resources," Musk claimed in a post.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Social media might be making you sweat—literally
item.description: By now everyone knows that scrolling social media isn’t exactly good for you. But did you know it might be making you sweat? Researchers from the psychology department at Durham University tracked the physiological responses of scrolling on social media and found a rather strange side effect.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Fortran, Delphi rise in Tiobe popularity index
item.description: Older programming languages such as Fortran and Delphi are making a comeback, based on the results of this month’s Tiobe index of language popularity. Tiobe attributes their rise to the need to maintain vital legacy systems still dependent on them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Voice-cloning companies hit for lack of safeguards against scammers
item.description: Companies creating generative AI (genAI) tools that can clone voices are not doing enough to prevent scammers from using them to defraud customers, according to a study from Consumer Reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Got a bank transfer alert text? It might be a scam; here’s what to do
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips to safeguard your personal information as scammers become more sophisticated with scam texts and other methods.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese humanoid robot lands world's first front flip
item.description: A robotics firm in China claims a robot has performed the world's first humanoid robot front flip, which is significantly more difficult than a backflip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lenovo introduces compact, liquid-cooled AI edge server
item.description: Lenovo has announced the ThinkEdge SE100, an entry-level AI inferencing server that’s designed to make edge AI affordable for enterprises as well as small and medium-sized businesses. AI systems are not normally small and compact; they’re big, decked out servers with lots of memory, GPUs, and CPUs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Duckduckgo launches its version of an AI search assistant
item.description: After nearly two years of intensive beta testing, Duckduckgo has launched the live version of the AI assistant previously known as Duck Assist. Similar to Google’s AI Overviews, the AI Assistant can provide quick answers to users’ questions, such as why Pluto is no longer classified as a planet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — The Albanian Cabinet decided on Thursday to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the popular video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Seven important trends in the server sphere
item.description: The pace of change around server technology is advancing considerably, driven by hyperscalers but spilling over into the on-premises world as well.
item.dc:creator: Network World
